name: pz-rekormonitor
documentation:
# Python Rekor Monitor

This tool allows one to validate entries in the Sigstore Rekor transparency log.

## Usage

The tool has three distinct flows.

1. **Checkpoint retreival**: Run `python-rekor-monitor` with the `-c` argument to
   obtain the latest checkpoint information from the server.

2. **Inclusion verification**: Run`python-rekor-monitor` with the following
   arguments:
     - `--inclusion <log_index>` with the specified Rekor log index to verify
       inclusion at.
     - `--artifact <artifact_filepath>` with the filepath to the artifact to
       verify inclusion of.

3. **Consistency verification**: Run `python-rekor-monitor` with the following
   arguments:
     - `--consistency` to enable this mode.
     - `--tree-id <tree_id>` with the specified Rekor tree ID to check.
     - `--tree-size <tree_size>` with the specified Rekor tree size to check.
     - `--root-hash <root_hash>` with the specified Rekor root hash to check.

The `--debug` flag may be added to any flow to view additional output useful for
debugging the monitor tool.

modules.name: pz_rekormonitor.util
documentation: Utilities for working with certificates and signatures.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.util.extract_public_key
documentation:
Extracts and returns the public key from the given certificate.

Args:
    cert (str): The certificate, in PEM format.

Returns:
    str: The public key.
extracts and returns public key from a given cert (in pem format)

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.util.verify_artifact_signature
documentation:
Verifies the signature of the provided artifact.

Args:
    signature (str): The signature to verify.
    public_key (str): The public key to verify the signature against.
    artifact_filename (str): The artifact to verify.

modules.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof
documentation: Tools for working with Merkle trees.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.verify_consistency
documentation:
Verifies the consistency of the tree.

Args:
    hasher (Hasher): The hasher to use.
    size1 (int): The size of the 1st tree.
    size2 (int): The size of the 2nd tree.
    proof (str[]): The data containing the proof for the tree.
    roots (str[]): The roots of the 1st and 2nd tree.

Raises:
    ValueError: Raised if size2 < size1.
    ValueError: Raised if size1 == size2, but there is proof data in proof.
    ValueError: Raised if an empty proof array was expected, but was not.
    ValueError: Raised if proof is empty when it should not have been.
    ValueError: Raised if proof is of the wrong size for the computed trees.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.verify_match
documentation:
Checks if the calculated root == the expected root.

Args:
    calculated (str): The calculated root hash.
    expected (str): The expected root hash.

Raises:
    RootMismatchError: Raised if the hashes do not match.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.decomp_incl_proof
documentation:
Decomposes an inclusion proof.

Args:
    index (int): The index of the proof.
    size (int): The size of the proof.
Returns:
    (int, int): The decomposed proof.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.inner_proof_size
documentation:
Returns the size of the inner proof.

Args:
    index (int): The index of the proof.
    size (int): The size of the proof.

Returns:
    int: The size of the proof.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.chain_inner
documentation: Chains inner proofs together.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.chain_inner_right
documentation: Chain inner proofs together.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.chain_border_right
documentation: Chain proofs together.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.root_from_inclusion_proof
documentation:
Compute the root from an inclusion proof.

Args:
    hasher (Hasher): The hasher to use.
    index (int): The index to use.
    size (int): The size to use.
    leaf_hash (str): The leaf hash.
    proof (str): The proof to use.

Raises:
    ValueError: Raised if the index is greater than the size.
    ValueError: Raised if the leaf_hash has an unexpected size.
    ValueError: Raised if the proof is of the wrong size.

Returns:
    str: The root hash.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.verify_inclusion
documentation:
Verify inclusion of an entry.

Args:
    hasher (Hasher): The hasher to use.
    index (int): The index to use.
    size (int): The size to use.
    leaf_hash (str): The leaf hash.
    proof (str[]): The proof to use.
    root (str): The hash of the root.
    debug (bool, optional): Print out debugging information. Defaults to False.
def verify_inclusion(hasher, index, size, leaf_hash, proof, root, debug=False):

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.compute_leaf_hash
documentation:
Computes the hash of a leaf.

Args:
    body (str): The data to be hashed.

Returns:
    str: The computed hash.
requires entry["body"] output for a log entry
returns the leaf hash according to the rfc 6962 spec

documentation: Returns a new instance of the Hasher

methods.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.Hasher.hash_leaf
documentation: Hashes the provided leaf

methods.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.Hasher.hash_children
documentation: Hashes the provided leaf + root

methods.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.Hasher.size
documentation: Returns the size of the digest
A representation of a Hasher.

classes.name: pz_rekormonitor.merkle_proof.RootMismatchError
documentation: An exception for the mismatch of roots

modules.name: pz_rekormonitor.main
documentation: A set of tools to interact with the Sigstore Rekor transparency log.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.main.get_log_entry
documentation:
Obtain the specified entry from Rekor.

Args:
    log_index (int): The index of the entry to obtain. Must be a positive int.
    debug (bool, optional): Print out debugging information. Defaults to False.

Raises:
    TypeError: Raised on an invalid type for log_index.
    ValueError: Raised on a negative value for log_index.
    SystemExit: Raised on an exception when sending the HTTP request to Rekor.
    SystemExit: Raised on an unexpected HTTP status code from Rekor.

Returns:
    str: The JSON payload from Rekor about the entry.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.main.get_verification_proof
documentation:
Get a verification proof for the specified entry in Rekor.

Args:
    log_index (int): The index of the entry to obtain. Must be a positive int.
    debug (bool, optional): Print out debugging information. Defaults to False.

Raises:
    TypeError: Raised on an invalid type for log_index.
    ValueError: Raised on a negative value for log_index.

Returns:
    (int, int, str, str[], str): The tuple containing the data needed for a proof.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.main.inclusion
documentation:
Checks if the specified artifact is included in Rekor.

Args:
    log_index (int): The index of the checkpoint to check for inclusion against. Must be a
                        positive int.
    artifact_filepath (str): The path to the artifact to check.
    debug (bool, optional): Print out debugging information. Defaults to False.

Raises:
    TypeError: Raised on an invalid type for log_index.
    ValueError: Raised on a negative value for log_index.
    TypeError: Raised on an invalid type for artifact_filepath.
    SystemExit: Raised on being unable to open the artifact file.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.main.get_latest_checkpoint
documentation:
Obtain the latest checkpoint from Rekor.

Args:
    debug (bool, optional): Print out debugging information. Defaults to False.

Raises:
    SystemExit: Raised on an exception when sending the HTTP request to Rekor.
    SystemExit: Raised on an unexpected HTTP status code from Rekor.

Returns:
    _type_: _description_

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.main.consistency
documentation:
_summary_

Args:
    prev_checkpoint (dict): The previous checkpoint to verify against.
    debug (bool, optional): Print out debugging information. Defaults to False.

Raises:
    ValueError: Raised on an invalid value for prev_checkpoint.
    SystemExit: Raised on an exception when sending the HTTP request to Rekor.
    SystemExit: Raised on an unexpected HTTP status code from Rekor.

functions.name: pz_rekormonitor.main.main
documentation: The main code for the tool that parses args and calls out to other functions
